# AGENT RULES — MANDATORY

These rules apply to EVERY agent working in this repo, regardless of model or provider.
Violation = incomplete work.

## 1. Work Ledger is Source of Truth

- This repo has a `work-ledger.md` in the root. It tracks all active tasks.
- Your task prompt (in `plans/{ITEM-ID}.md`) was generated from the master ledger.
- When you finish work, update `work-ledger.md` — not just your task file.

## 2. Status Updates Are Required

Before you report "done", you MUST update `work-ledger.md`:

1. Find your item's section (matching `## {ITEM-ID}`)
2. Under `### Status Updates`, append:
   ```
   - {YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM} — STATUS: built — {1-line summary of what you did}
   ```
3. Fill in verification bullets:
   ```
   - [x] What was changed
   - [x] How to verify on live site
   - [x] Edge cases or follow-ups
   ```

If you skip this step, your work will be marked incomplete regardless of code quality.

## 3. New Work → New Ledger Item

If your task spawns follow-ups, blockers, or new priorities:

1. Append a new section to `work-ledger.md` using the exact format of existing items
2. Use a temporary ID: `AUTO-{YYYYMMDD}-{SHORT-DESCRIPTION}` (e.g., `AUTO-20260301-FEED-EDGE-CASE`)
3. Set status to `pending` and priority to `50` (default)
4. Next sync will pull it into the master ledger automatically

## 4. Branch Safety

- You are on `ci-testing`. Never commit to `main` or `production`.
- If you need to read `main` for reference, that's fine. Never write to it.

## 5. Files You Should Know About

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `work-ledger.md` | Your task list + status tracker |
| `plans/{ITEM-ID}.md` | Full task prompt with context |
| `codebase_map.json` | Auto-generated function/schema index |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude-specific conventions (if present) |

## 6. Verification Standard

"Done" means a real user can do the thing on the live site. Not "code compiles."
Not "tests pass." If you can't verify end-to-end, say so in your status update.

## 7. Every Task Maps to a North Star

Every work ledger item, plan, and dispatch must declare which North Star Objective it advances:
- **NSO-01** — Payment Works Everywhere
- **NSO-02** — Community Is Stickier Than Any Feature
- **NSO-03** — Every Happy User Recruits One More
- **NSO-04** — Kai Runs the Machine
- **NSO-05** — Top of Funnel Is Always Full
- **NSO-06** — Zero Human Barriers Mid-Execution
- **NSO-07** — Client Relationships Are Grounded in Mutual Accountability

Items without a mapping are excluded from planning selection. If you can't map it, ask before proceeding.
Enforced by: Rule `ops-objective-alignment-required`

## 8. No Human Barriers Mid-Execution

Human approval is only valid at the **final publish/deploy gate**. Mid-execution human gates are architectural debt — redesign the flow.

The ONLY 3 exceptions where a human may gate mid-flow:
1. Direct money spend (new cost being authorized)
2. Public-facing publish or deploy (code going live)
3. Dramatic change to a critical user flow

Everything else: advance autonomously. Do not wait for Nick to decide HOW to execute.
Enforced by: Rule `ops-no-human-mid-execution`

## 9. Operational Memory (OpenViking / ChromaDB)

A semantic vector memory layer stores past outcomes, incidents, rules, and daily context.
Before planning or diagnosing, check memory for prior context:

```bash
# Pre-plan memory brief — finds similar outcomes and known dead ends
python3 ~/scceo-1/openviking/scripts/pre_plan_memory.py --domain "DOMAIN" --title "TITLE" --format markdown

# Health check
python3 ~/scceo-1/openviking/scripts/memory_health.py
```

Direct file access (no scripts needed):
- Today's overview: `~/scceo-1/exports/openviking/daily/YYYY-MM-DD/overview.md`
- Past outcomes: `~/scceo-1/exports/openviking/outcomes/`
- Active issues: `~/scceo-1/exports/openviking/megafix/`
- Latest health: `~/scceo-1/openviking/logs/health-latest.json`
